title: ferray-random: distributions don't accept array parameters — no broadcasting
description:
Goal: 

Acceptance criteria:
- 

NumPy's distributions accept array parameters: rng.normal(loc=[0,1,2], scale=[1,2,3]) generates one sample per element, broadcasting loc and scale together. ferray-random requires scalar parameters for all distributions. There's no way to generate samples with different parameters per element without a loop. NumPy uses broadcasting internally (via np.broadcast) to handle array parameters. This is important for: different variance per feature, varying rate parameters in Poisson processes, etc.
